food_subgroup: Pastry tarts and tartlets — assorted flavours gift set

ontology.foodon_gap_note: FoodOn provides a generic tart category. No specific code exists for an assorted mini tart gift set with lemon meringue, raspberry, and sea salt chocolate flavours. Closest parent used.
ontology.foodex2_gap_note: FoodEx2 A0FKP covers pastry products broadly. No specific code exists for assorted mini tarts or gift-set confectionery tarts. Closest parent used.
ontology.snomed_ct_gap_note: No suitable SNOMED CT code identified for assorted mini pastry tarts gift set. SNOMED CT clinical coverage does not extend to this specific commercial confectionery product.

main_ingredients: pastry shell (wheat flour, butter, sugar, eggs)

serving.natural_language_portion: One mini tart from the gift set weighs approximately 30 g; the full gift set contains 9 mini tarts (3 lemon meringue, 3 raspberry, 3 sea salt chocolate) totalling approximately 270 g.

nutrient_source.title: Tart, fruit, commercially prepared — USDA FoodData Central SR Legacy (NDB 18413) and Chocolate tart / pastry shell composite estimation
nutrient_source.organization: USDA Agricultural Research Service — FoodData Central
nutrient_source.source_match_notes: No exact nutritional entry exists for Kazo Galaxy Mini Tarts Gift Set in any approved primary source. Values are estimated as a weighted composite of three flavour variants: (1) lemon meringue tart approximated from USDA FoodData Central 'Pie, lemon meringue, commercially prepared' (FDC ID 172031); (2) raspberry tart approximated from USDA FoodData Central 'Pie, fruit, two crust, commercially prepared' adjusted for raspberry filling; (3) sea salt chocolate tart approximated from USDA FoodData Central 'Pie, chocolate creme, commercially prepared' (FDC ID 172033). Equal weighting applied across three flavours. Sodium adjusted upward to reflect sea salt component in chocolate variant. All values are per 100 g of the blended product. Confidence is medium due to estimation from analogous USDA entries.

gi_source.title: Glycemic index of pastry and sweet baked goods — analogous estimate based on 'Pastry, flaky' and 'Tart shell, sweet shortcrust' entries
gi_source.source_match_notes: No direct GI measurement exists for Kazo Galaxy Mini Tarts. GI estimated at 55 (low) based on analogous sweet shortcrust pastry tart entries in the University of Sydney GI Database. Sweet shortcrust pastry tarts with fruit and chocolate fillings typically fall in the GI range 50–60 due to high fat and sugar content moderating glucose response. Glycemic load per serving calculated as: (GI × carbohydrate_g per serving) / 100 = (55 × 14.4) / 100 = 7.9, where one 30 g mini tart contains approximately 14.4 g carbohydrate (48.0 g per 100 g × 0.30).

hypertension.guidance: Each mini tart contributes approximately 54 mg sodium; the sea salt chocolate variant adds additional sodium, so individuals managing hypertension should limit intake to one or two pieces and monitor total daily sodium from all sources.

type2_diabetes.key_factor: High refined carbohydrate and sugar content with low glycemic index
type2_diabetes.guidance: With approximately 14.4 g of carbohydrate and 8.4 g of sugar per 30 g mini tart and a low GI of 55, individuals with Type 2 Diabetes should restrict consumption to one piece per occasion and pair with a low-GI food to blunt postprandial glucose excursion, as the overall carbohydrate load is still significant.

cardiovascular_disease.key_factor: Saturated fat, trans fat, and cholesterol content
cardiovascular_disease.guidance: Each 30 g mini tart delivers approximately 2.9 g saturated fat and 0.06 g trans fat; individuals with cardiovascular disease should limit consumption to occasional small portions and prioritise unsaturated fat sources in the remainder of their diet.

llm_training.natural_language_description: The Kazo Galaxy Mini Tarts Gift Set is a premium artisan pastry gift set sold by Kazo Premium at Funan Mall in Singapore. The set contains nine individually crafted mini tarts in three flavours: three Lemon Meringue Mini Tarts, three Raspberry Mini Tarts, and three Sea Salt Chocolate Mini Tarts. Each mini tart features a buttery sweet shortcrust pastry shell baked to a golden, crisp finish and filled with a distinct flavoured filling. The Lemon Meringue variant is filled with a tangy lemon curd and topped with a lightly torched Italian or Swiss meringue, offering a balance of citrus acidity and sweet creaminess. The Raspberry Mini Tart is filled with a vibrant raspberry compote or fresh raspberry curd, delivering a bright, fruity tartness against the rich pastry base. The Sea Salt Chocolate Mini Tart features a dark chocolate ganache filling finished with a sprinkle of fleur de sel or coarse sea salt, creating a sophisticated sweet-savoury contrast. The gift set is presented in decorative packaging suitable for gifting occasions such as birthdays, corporate events, and festive celebrations. Nutritionally, each mini tart (approximately 30 g) provides around 111 kcal, 5.4 g fat, 2.9 g saturated fat, 14.4 g carbohydrate, and 8.4 g sugar. The low glycemic index (estimated 55) combined with moderate sugar load makes this a treat-category food that individuals with Type 2 Diabetes or cardiovascular risk should consume in limited quantities. The sea salt component in the chocolate variant contributes additional sodium, which is relevant for individuals managing hypertension. The set retails at SGD 29.00 and is available via GrabFood Singapore from Kazo Premium at Funan.
common_user_queries: What is in the Kazo Galaxy Mini Tarts Gift Set?
How many tarts are in the Kazo gift set?
Kazo sea salt chocolate tart sodium
Is Kazo mini tart suitable for diabetics?

confidence_reason: No exact nutritional entry for Kazo Galaxy Mini Tarts Gift Set exists in any approved primary source (HPB Singapore Foodo, USDA FoodData Central, or ICMR-NIN). Nutrient values are estimated as a weighted composite from three analogous USDA FoodData Central entries for commercially prepared lemon meringue pie, fruit pie, and chocolate cream pie, adjusted to a per-100 g basis for mini tart format. GI is an analogous estimate from the University of Sydney GI Database for sweet shortcrust pastry tarts. Confidence is therefore medium.
notes: 1. Nutrient values are estimated from USDA FoodData Central analogous entries (lemon meringue pie FDC 172031, chocolate cream pie FDC 172033, fruit pie composite) weighted equally across three flavour variants. Direct laboratory analysis of the Kazo product would be required for high-confidence values. 2. vitamin_b6_mg is -1 as it is not reported in the analogous USDA source entries used for estimation. 3. vitamin_b12_mcg is -1 as it is not reported in the analogous USDA source entries used for estimation. 4. folate_mcg_dfe is -1 as it is not reported in the analogous USDA source entries used for estimation. 5. vitamin_d_mcg is -1 as it is not reported in the analogous USDA source entries used for estimation. 6. vitamin_e_mg is -1 as it is not reported in the analogous USDA source entries used for estimation. 7. selenium_mcg is -1 as it is not reported in the analogous USDA source entries used for estimation. 8. GI of 55 is an analogous estimate from the University of Sydney GI Database for sweet shortcrust pastry tarts; no direct GI measurement for Kazo Galaxy Mini Tarts exists. 9. Halal status is not confirmed from available sources; is_halal is set to false pending verification from Kazo Premium directly. 10. The food_group has been reclassified from 'Mixed' (input) to 'Desserts' as the product is a pastry dessert gift set. 11. Serving size set at 30 g per mini tart (one piece) as a natural consumption unit; the full set is 270 g (9 tarts).

Added multilingual local names for all four mandatory Singaporean languages (Chinese simplified/traditional, Malay, Tamil, Hindi). Estimated nutrient values from USDA FoodData Central analogous entries. Added GI estimate from University of Sydney GI Database analogous entries. Populated health_context for Hypertension, Type 2 Diabetes, and Cardiovascular Disease. Documented all -1 sentinel values in notes. Verified aliases_by_language consistency with flat aliases list.

Corrected `gi_category` from 'medium' to 'low' as glycemic_index 55 falls into the 'low' category (<= 55) per University of Sydney GI Database guidelines. Updated `gi_source.source_match_notes` and `health_context.type2_diabetes.guidance` to reflect the corrected 'low' GI category. Updated `veritas_meta` and `veritas_qc` accordingly.
